questions.question: What is K-Means clustering?
keyPoints: K-Means clustering is a type of unsupervised machine learning algorithm
It is used to group similar data points into clusters based on their features

questions.question: What is the goal of K-Means clustering?
keyPoints: The goal of K-Means clustering is to identify patterns or structures in the data
It is used to group similar data points into clusters based on their features

questions.question: What are the advantages of K-Means clustering?
keyPoints: K-Means clustering is simple to implement
It is efficient and effective for spherical clusters

questions.question: What are the disadvantages of K-Means clustering?
keyPoints: K-Means clustering is sensitive to initial conditions
It can get stuck in local optima

questions.question: How can K-Means clustering be improved?
keyPoints: K-Means clustering can be improved by using techniques such as feature scaling
Data normalization and ensemble methods can also be used

questions.question: Explain the difference between K-Means clustering and hierarchical clustering.
keyPoints: K-Means clustering is a flat clustering algorithm
Hierarchical clustering is a hierarchical algorithm

questions.question: Explain the difference between K-Means clustering and DBSCAN clustering.
keyPoints: K-Means clustering is a centroid-based clustering algorithm
DBSCAN clustering is a density-based clustering algorithm

questions.question: Explain the advantages and disadvantages of K-Means clustering.
keyPoints: K-Means clustering is simple to implement and efficient
It is sensitive to initial conditions and can get stuck in local optima
